title: campanula lezgina
intro: Campanula lezgina, also known as Lezgina bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campanula lezgina include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Campanula lezgina often include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or by boiling them in a tea.
